---
title: "ODA basics with oda_fit()"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

**Purpose:** Introduce `oda_fit()` for binary-class ODA. Cover the attribute,
class variable, cutpoint, rule direction, PAC per class, Mean PAC, ESS, LOO
validity, and the chance benchmark. Use a worked example to show how to read
and interpret output fields.

**Source material:** `vignettes/migraine-attacks-oda.Rmd` (expand and
annotate); MPE MegaODA training examples 1–4
(`tests/testthat/fixtures/vignettes/data/`).

**Eval policy:** Live, CRAN-safe. Small `mc_iter` (≤ 500) for all evaluated
chunks. Canonical mc_iter = 25000 results stated as text, not live-computed.

---

*This article is not yet expanded. Placeholder sections are listed below.*

## The ODA problem

## Fitting with `oda_fit()`

## Reading the output: rule, PAC, ESS

## Leave-one-out (LOO) validity

## Notes on MC p-value calibration
